_comment: Go counterpart of benchmark_queries.json. Same query shapes and gold-module intent as the Python fixture, with expected_modules pointing at tests/fixtures/sample_project_go source paths. Used to measure top-k retrieval hit-rate per language (issue #173, E2.3 / E2.4).

queries.question: How does authentication work in this codebase?
What are the main API endpoints?
Explain the billing flow from a user perspective.
How are users stored in the database?
How does JWT signature verification work?
What happens when a Stripe webhook fires?
How do I create a new user?
What database does this project use?
How are invoices sent to users?
A user login is failing without any error message — which file contains credential validation?
Stripe webhooks are being rejected — where is the signature verified?
Invoices are not appearing for a user — which files handle invoice retrieval?
Users are being logged out unexpectedly — where is token expiry enforced?
I need to add an email_verified column to users — which files would change?
I need to replace the database backend — which file owns the connection pool?
I want to add multi-factor authentication to the login flow — which files would I modify?
I just changed how JWT tokens are signed — what other files should I review?
I updated the charge_customer function — what else commonly changes with billing?

learning_seed._comment: Queries used to seed the memory log before the learning-uplift phase. Heavier on auth + billing than on users, to create a realistic cooccurrence signal the reranker can exploit.
history: How does the JWT token get signed?
Can a user log in with an expired token?
How do I charge a customer?
What happens if a Stripe charge fails?
How do I issue a partial refund?
How does the login endpoint work?
How is a user session revoked?
